mechanism_narrative: Rising civilian employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market strength, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This trend is persistent and always active.
failure_condition: If monthly PAYEMS growth rate is negative while SPX still rises, or if rolling 12-month correlation between PAYEMS changes and SPX returns is less than 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4%, it indicates an extremely tight labor market that forces wage inflation and preempts Fed tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative short-term returns. The signal activates only when the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than half of instances when UNRATE is below 4%, or if the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) determines the real interest rate. When real rates (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) are negative, monetary conditions are accommodative, supporting equity prices on a 60-day horizon. The joint behavior of both series is required.
failure_condition: If, in periods of negative real rates, S&P 500 60-day returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0) or if the rank IC between real rate changes and returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, each increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate on future earnings, reducing equity valuations. This mechanism is persistent throughout tightening periods and depresses forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Federal Reserve initiates an easing cycle while the funds rate is still above 2%, or if the average 20-day return following FEDFUNDS hikes is positive in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending, measured by a month-over-month rise in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 0.5%, signals robust aggregate demand that drives corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following strong retail sales releases is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or if the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) rises above 105, it signals capacity constraints and overheating in the manufacturing sector, which often precedes a slowdown as supply cannot keep pace with demand, leading to negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of episodes when INDPRO exceeds 105, or if the average return is greater than 0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint rise in housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates synchronized expansion in construction and labor markets, which historically leads to sustained economic growth and positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon. The signal requires both series to be above their respective thresholds simultaneously.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are not positive in at least 60% of periods when both thresholds are met, or if the average return is less than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) is rising steadily, and its level above the previous year's level signals ongoing economic expansion. This always-on trend supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal is always active.
failure_condition: If, during periods of real GDP growth, SPX 60-day returns are negative more than 40% of the time, or if the correlation between GDP level and subsequent returns is non-positive over any 5-year window.
